delta.text: Found the imm
ediate crash bug. Let me also check codegen since the JMP encoded looks like NO_JUMP (-1 with

content.text: Found the immediate crash bug. Let me also check codegen since the JMP encoded looks like NO_JUMP (-1 with proper bias).

content.content:
Found 1 file
crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs

delta.text: Let me check `test_then_block` and `ifstat` to see if the JMP is being patched.

content.text: Let me check `test_then_block` and `ifstat` to see if the JMP is being patched.

delta.text: Decoded clearly. The bug: `arg_s_j` in `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.r
s:176` uses the wrong bias `0x1FFFF` (that's the sBx bias) — for the
25-bit sJ field the correct bias is `0xFFFFFF` (per `crates/lua-code/src/opcodes.rs:99`). The J
MP raw 0x7FFFFF38 decodes (with correct bias) to sJ=-1 (NO_JUMP); with the wrong bias it
yields +16646143, causing the out-of-bounds.

content.text: Decoded clearly. The bug: `arg_s_j` in `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:176` uses the wrong bias `0x1FFFF` (that's the sBx bias) — for the 25-bit sJ field the correct bias is `0xFFFFFF` (per `crates/lua-code/src/opcodes.rs:99`). The JMP raw 0x7FFFFF38 decodes (with correct bias) to sJ=-1 (NO_JUMP); with the wrong bias it yields +16646143, causing the out-of-bounds.

delta.text: Let me look at the codegen issue too, given that JMP isn't being patched is a separate bug.

content.text: Let me look at the codegen issue too, given that JMP isn't being patched is a separate bug.

delta.text: The original panic at `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1426:22` is gone. With
the bias fix, the JMP raw `0x7FFFFF38` decodes correctly to
sJ = -1 (the NO_JUMP sentinel) instead of +16,646,143
. The new blocker is downstream: codegen emits a JMP with the NO_JUMP sentinel that never gets patched, because
`test_then_block` in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:2895` has T
ODO stubs for `go_if_true`/`go_if_false` instead of real calls — so the program now inf

inite-loops on the unpatched JMP. That's a separate, much larger bug for the next loop iteration.

## Report

**Root cause**
— `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:176`: `fn arg_s_j(&self) -> i32 { self.arg_ax() - 0
x1FFFF }`. The sJ field is the full 25-bit Ax field; its bias is `OFFSET_S_J = M
AXARG_S_J >> 1 = 0xFFFFFF` (see `crates/lua-code/src/opcodes.rs:99`). The code
used `0x1FFFF` (the 17-bit sBx bias) by mistake. The JMP raw `0x7FFFFF38` has ax=0x
FFFFFE; with the wrong bias this decoded as +16,646,143, so `pc = 4 + 16,646,143 + 1
= 16,646,148` indexed an 11-entry `proto.code`

.

**Fix** — changed bias to `0xFFFFFF` in the one offending
line of `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs`. Also removed the temporary `eprintln!` instrumentation I added to `state.rs::

proto_code`.

**New blocker (next loop iteration)** — the program now hangs instead of panicking. With the corre
ct bias, the JMP emitted by `codeorder` (lib.rs:791) decodes to sJ = -1 (NO_JUMP), conf
irming it was never patched. `test_then_block` (lib.rs:2895) has `// TODO(port): lua_code::go_if_true(...)` stubs at
lines 2910/2927 — the conditional-jump patching for `if`/`elseif` simply isn't wired up.
That's a sizable codegen port, not a one-liner.

content.text:
The original panic at `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1426:22` is gone. With the bias fix, the JMP raw `0x7FFFFF38` decodes correctly to sJ = -1 (the NO_JUMP sentinel) instead of +16,646,143. The new blocker is downstream: codegen emits a JMP with the NO_JUMP sentinel that never gets patched, because `test_then_block` in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:2895` has TODO stubs for `go_if_true`/`go_if_false` instead of real calls — so the program now infinite-loops on the unpatched JMP. That's a separate, much larger bug for the next loop iteration.

## Report

**Root cause** — `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:176`: `fn arg_s_j(&self) -> i32 { self.arg_ax() - 0x1FFFF }`. The sJ field is the full 25-bit Ax field; its bias is `OFFSET_S_J = MAXARG_S_J >> 1 = 0xFFFFFF` (see `crates/lua-code/src/opcodes.rs:99`). The code used `0x1FFFF` (the 17-bit sBx bias) by mistake. The JMP raw `0x7FFFFF38` has ax=0xFFFFFE; with the wrong bias this decoded as +16,646,143, so `pc = 4 + 16,646,143 + 1 = 16,646,148` indexed an 11-entry `proto.code`.

**Fix** — changed bias to `0xFFFFFF` in the one offending line of `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs`. Also removed the temporary `eprintln!` instrumentation I added to `state.rs::proto_code`.

**New blocker (next loop iteration)** — the program now hangs instead of panicking. With the correct bias, the JMP emitted by `codeorder` (lib.rs:791) decodes to sJ = -1 (NO_JUMP), confirming it was never patched. `test_then_block` (lib.rs:2895) has `// TODO(port): lua_code::go_if_true(...)` stubs at lines 2910/2927 — the conditional-jump patching for `if`/`elseif` simply isn't wired up. That's a sizable codegen port, not a one-liner.

result:
The original panic at `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1426:22` is gone. With the bias fix, the JMP raw `0x7FFFFF38` decodes correctly to sJ = -1 (the NO_JUMP sentinel) instead of +16,646,143. The new blocker is downstream: codegen emits a JMP with the NO_JUMP sentinel that never gets patched, because `test_then_block` in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:2895` has TODO stubs for `go_if_true`/`go_if_false` instead of real calls — so the program now infinite-loops on the unpatched JMP. That's a separate, much larger bug for the next loop iteration.

## Report

**Root cause** — `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:176`: `fn arg_s_j(&self) -> i32 { self.arg_ax() - 0x1FFFF }`. The sJ field is the full 25-bit Ax field; its bias is `OFFSET_S_J = MAXARG_S_J >> 1 = 0xFFFFFF` (see `crates/lua-code/src/opcodes.rs:99`). The code used `0x1FFFF` (the 17-bit sBx bias) by mistake. The JMP raw `0x7FFFFF38` has ax=0xFFFFFE; with the wrong bias this decoded as +16,646,143, so `pc = 4 + 16,646,143 + 1 = 16,646,148` indexed an 11-entry `proto.code`.

**Fix** — changed bias to `0xFFFFFF` in the one offending line of `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs`. Also removed the temporary `eprintln!` instrumentation I added to `state.rs::proto_code`.

**New blocker (next loop iteration)** — the program now hangs instead of panicking. With the correct bias, the JMP emitted by `codeorder` (lib.rs:791) decodes to sJ = -1 (NO_JUMP), confirming it was never patched. `test_then_block` (lib.rs:2895) has `// TODO(port): lua_code::go_if_true(...)` stubs at lines 2910/2927 — the conditional-jump patching for `if`/`elseif` simply isn't wired up. That's a sizable codegen port, not a one-liner.
